adjusted.reasoning: Argentina won Group J with nine points and Messi has scored all five of their goals; Cape Verde came through on three draws. Heavy favourites (-650). Model's 97% trimmed to the market for knockout variance.

factors.factor: Argentina -650 / Cape Verde +1900; Messi 5 goals; ARG 9pts

posted_to.note: R32 pre-bet sweep (whole round biddable). PROTECT mode (rank 1/34, lead +12, rem_grp=0) but k=0.3 => contest pick == EP-max on adjusted track; ~32 KO matches remain so EP-max is right. Winner-scoreline (KO; platform draw odds 0.00). Anchored to de-vigged market.

Re-bet 2:0 -> 1:0 after fixing KO scoring to the community's 'after penalties' rule (a level game resolves to a 1-goal win for the shootout winner). Under the corrected EP model 1:0 beats 2:0 for strong favourites (0-0->pens becomes an exact 1-0). Pre-kickoff, prediction unchanged.

adjusted.reasoning: Colombia won Group K and are favourites (-135), but the market is far more cautious than the model's 96% — Ghana are organised and earned four group points. Anchor firmly to market (calibration has the market beating the model on these big-favourite knockouts).

factors.factor: Colombia -135 / draw +250 / Ghana +410; COL -300 to advance; NBC COL 2-1

adjusted.reasoning: Egypt are slight favourites (+150) on Salah's star power, but his hamstring is in doubt after the Iran draw; Australia are compact and set-piece dangerous (0-0 with Paraguay). Very open, high draw; anchor to market with a small shade off Egypt for the Salah doubt.

factors.factor: Egypt +150 / draw +200 / Australia +225; Salah hamstring doubt; Australia compact

odds.source: DK/Polymarket 2026-06-27 (EGY +150/draw +200/AUS +225); Egypt fav but Salah hamstring doubt; AUS compact
